actionable_feedback: Provide the missing limit_chip_hotspot_C value in the embedded packet or explicitly cite the authoritative source for the proxy you used. If the value is unknown, include a short rationale for chosen proxy and mark as provisional in both the workbook and memo.

Embed the normalized_risk formula as visible cell formulas in the workbook AND include the exact plain-text formula block in facilities_action.md (show the input fields, numerator/denominator, and example calculation for one record).

Ensure the workbook is saved in an openpyxl-readable .xlsx format and/or include CSV exports of the key sheets (Summary, Records, Checks) so automated graders can parse values and formulas. Confirm that formulas are present and not only cached values.

Add one manual recomputation example in facilities_action.md: list the record inputs, limits, formula steps, and resulting normalized_risk rounded to at least two decimal places to match grader expectations.
Re-run the sensitivity +/-5% checks and show the recomputed numeric results with at least two decimal places in both the workbook and memo.
Label any provisional data clearly in both workbook cells and the memo, and include a short provenance note describing where each limit and proxy came from.
artifact_specific_comments: facilities_action.md: Clear narrative, includes per-record numeric results, sensitivity checks, and the recommendation table. Good candidate to host the plain-text formula and manual recomputation example.
liquid_cooling_stability.xlsx: Contains formulas and cached values per trace, but environment preview failed with openpyxl_unavailable. Re-save or add CSV exports to ensure machine readability.
failed_or_weak_rubric_items: reproducibility_missing_limit: Missing limit_chip_hotspot_C required a provisional proxy, reducing reproducibility.
xlsx_parsing_unavailable: Workbook could not be programmatically opened in the grader environment, preventing automated formula inspection.
failed_rubric_items: reproducibility_missing_limit: Missing limit_chip_hotspot_C required a provisional proxy, reducing reproducibility.
xlsx_parsing_unavailable: Workbook could not be programmatically opened in the grader environment, preventing automated formula inspection.
feedback_summary: The submission meets the artifact contract and presents plausible per-record normalized_risk values, classifications, sensitivity checks, and a defensible minimal-action recommendation. Two reproducibility/witnessability issues reduce full confidence: (1) the embedded packet omitted limit_chip_hotspot_C so a provisional proxy was used (this is flagged in the memo but prevents exact recomputation), and (2) the XLSX could not be programmatically opened in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable), so automated formula inspection was not possible. Addressing these will restore full auditability and satisfy the evaluator guidance to preserve at least two decimal places when recomputing normalized_risk.

recommended_actions_summary: Include missing limit_chip_hotspot_C or justify proxy in packet and workbook.
Expose normalized_risk formula as plain text and visible cell formulas.
Provide openpyxl-readable workbook or CSV exports of key sheets and a manual recomputation example.
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Two warnings: xlsx parsing unavailable in preview environment and provisional proxy for missing limit_chip_hotspot_C reduces immediate reproducibility.
